offer.title: I build and run growth engines for industrial tech.
offer.subtitle: CEO with a track record of building and scaling industrial-tech businesses and turning around performance when needed through execution and systems.
points: Design sustainable GTM playbooks and partner routes that fit your market
Turnaround your operations and build efficient teams
Stabilize cash, supply, and delivery with pragmatic ops changes

about.headline: Building industrial technology businesses, from strategy to execution.
about.body:
My career has been shaped by building businesses under real-world conditions: new markets, limited resources, and high complexity.

Over the past 15+ years, I have built, scaled, and stabilized industrial companies across Europe, North America, and Asia — often in situations without a blueprint, where execution mattered more than theory.

I work hands-on at the intersection of market, organization, and technology: from go-to-market strategy and sales & supply-chain structures to operational systems such as CRM, ERP, and reporting. For me, strategy does not end with a concept — it ends with measurable results.

Outside of work, I find balance in sailing and hiking. Both strongly influence how I work: setting a clear course, planning ahead, navigating uncertainty, and making decisions as conditions change.
highlights: Built international industrial businesses from the ground up, including in the U.S., China, and APAC
Scaled revenue from $0.8M to $13.5M while achieving >15% EBITDA
Established customer-centric go-to-market models (NPS >60, NRR ~126%)
Designed and implemented custom CRM, ERP, and accounting systems for real operational workflows
Developed partner and distribution networks across North America, APAC, and MENA

personal.title: Away from the desk, I prefer weather, water, and open terrain.
personal.intro: Skiing, cycling, and hiking keep me outside, but sailing is the one I come back to most. On the water, plans matter, conditions change, and good decisions come from reading both the instruments and the horizon.

timeline.title: Founder & CEO, INTUTEC LLC
timeline.detail: Founded and built an industrial marketplace for automation components. Responsible for vision, product definition, data model, UI architecture, go-to-market, and manufacturer onboarding.
highlights: Translated fragmented industrial component data into a structured marketplace model.
Defined the product architecture, supplier onboarding flow, and commercial positioning.
Built the operating stack across product, data, sales process, and manufacturer relationships.

timeline.title: President, Schlegel USA Inc.
timeline.detail: Leading the U.S. organization of an HMI components manufacturer. Responsibility for strategy, business planning, cash flow management, forecasting, and partner development.
highlights: Own the U.S. business plan, forecasting cadence, cash flow discipline, and commercial priorities.
Develop partner structures for OEM, distributor, and strategic account growth.
Connect market feedback with product, supply, and operational decisions.

timeline.title: CEO & President, CAPTRON North America LP
timeline.detail: Turned around and scaled the business. Increased revenue from $0.8M (2017) to $13.5M (2021) while achieving >15% EBITDA. Localized the supply chain, improving OTIF from 56% to 88%. Designed and implemented a custom CRM, ERP, and accounting system.
highlights: Rebuilt the commercial engine, account focus, and forecasting rhythm for sustained growth.
Localized supply chain and fulfillment operations to improve OTIF from 56% to 88%.
Designed internal CRM, ERP, reporting, and accounting workflows to run the business with clearer data.

timeline.title: Head of Business Development, CAPTRON Electronic GmbH
timeline.detail: Built the NAFTA distribution network and grew revenue from $4k (2014) to $800k (2017). Developed new industry segments and product portfolios, generating more than $10M in additional global revenue.
highlights: Established NAFTA distribution coverage and sales routines from a near-zero base.
Identified new industrial segments and converted them into product and portfolio initiatives.
Built cross-functional momentum between sales, product, and international market development.

timeline.title: Regional Manager Asia, SATECO Asia Limited
timeline.detail: Led market entry into the Chinese automotive sector. Incorporated a FICE in Suzhou with a €600k investment budget and managed €4.5M in revenue across China, India, and Korea.
highlights: Led local market entry work for automotive OEM and Tier supplier opportunities.
Set up the Suzhou FICE structure and managed the investment budget and operating ramp.
Coordinated revenue, partners, and customer relationships across China, India, and Korea.

timeline.title: GM & Commercial Roles (Mentor, MBG, Eisbär)
timeline.detail: Held interim general management and commercial roles. Led purchasing, built supplier networks, and developed project pipelines across consumer and industrial electronics.
highlights: Combined purchasing, supplier development, and commercial responsibility in owner-managed environments.
Built sourcing networks and project pipelines across Europe and China.
Gained early general management experience in hands-on operating roles.

contact.title: Is a specific growth step on the horizon?
contact.intro: Whether it is new markets, digital sales, leaner processes, or clear operating structures, a short note is enough for a first conversation.
contact.directNote: Reach me by email, phone, LinkedIn, or schedule a short call.
contact.promptText: Send a short note with the context, and I will reply with a useful next step.
